XSLT/xsl:stylesheet/xpath-default-namesp.name: xpath-default-namespace
XSLT/xsl:stylesheet/xpath-default-namesp.description: Specifies the namespace that will be used if the element name is unprefixed or an unprefixed type name within an XPath expression.

info.excerpt: Specifies the namespace that will be used if the element name is unprefixed or an unprefixed type name within an XPath expression.

XSLT/xsl:apply-imports.name: xsl:apply-imports
XSLT/xsl:apply-imports.description: The<xsl:apply-imports>element is fairly arcane, used mostly in complex stylesheets. Import precedence requires that template rules in main stylesheets have higher precedence than template rules in imported stylesheets. Sometimes, however, it is useful to be able to force the processor to use a templ

info.excerpt: The<xsl:apply-imports>element is fairly arcane, used mostly in complex stylesheets. Import precedence requires that template rules in main stylesheets have higher precedence than template rules in imp...

XSLT/xsl:apply-templates.name: xsl:apply-templates
XSLT/xsl:apply-templates.description: The<xsl:apply-templates>element selects a set of nodes in the input tree and instructs the processor to apply the proper templates to them.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:apply-templates>element selects a set of nodes in the input tree and instructs the processor to apply the proper templates to them.

XSLT/xsl:attribute.name: xsl:attribute
XSLT/xsl:attribute.description: The<xsl:attribute>element creates an attribute in the output document, using any values that can be accessed from the stylesheet. The element must be defined before any other output document element inside the output document element for which it establishes attribute values. But it may be after or

info.excerpt: The<xsl:attribute>element creates an attribute in the output document, using any values that can be accessed from the stylesheet. The element must be defined before any other output document element i...

XSLT/xsl:attribute-set.name: xsl:attribute-set
XSLT/xsl:attribute-set.description: The<xsl:attribute-set>element creates a named set of attributes, which can then be applied as whole to the output document, in a manner similar to named styles in CSS.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:attribute-set>element creates a named set of attributes, which can then be applied as whole to the output document, in a manner similar to named styles in CSS.
The<xsl:call-template>element invokes a named template.

XSLT/xsl:choose.name: xsl:choose
signatures: <xsl:choose> <xsl:when test="[whatever to test1]"></xsl:when> <xsl:when test="[whatever to test2]"></xsl:when> <xsl:otherwise></xsl:otherwise> [
XSLT/xsl:choose.description: The<xsl:choose>element defines a choice among a number of alternatives. It behaves like a switch statement in procedural languages.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:choose>element defines a choice among a number of alternatives. It behaves like a switch statement in procedural languages.

XSLT/xsl:comment.name: xsl:comment
XSLT/xsl:comment.description: The<xsl:comment>element writes a comment to the output document. It must include only text.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:comment>element writes a comment to the output document. It must include only text.

XSLT/xsl:copy.name: xsl:copy
XSLT/xsl:copy.description: The<xsl:copy>element transfers a shallow copy (the node and any associated namespace node) of the current node to the output document. It does not copy any children or attributes of the current node.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:copy>element transfers a shallow copy (the node and any associated namespace node) of the current node to the output document. It does not copy any children or attributes of the current node.

XSLT/xsl:copy-of.name: xsl:copy-of
XSLT/xsl:copy-of.description: The<xsl:copy-of>element makes a deep copy (including descendant nodes) of whatever the select attribute specifies to the output document.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:copy-of>element makes a deep copy (including descendant nodes) of whatever the select attribute specifies to the output document.

XSLT/xsl:decimal-format.name: xsl:decimal-format
signatures: <xsl:decimal-format name=NAME decimal-separator=CHARACTER grouping-separator=CHARACTER infinity=STRING minus-sign=CHARACTER NaN=STRING p
XSLT/xsl:decimal-format.description: The<xsl:decimal-format>element defines the characters and symbols that are to be used in converting numbers into strings using theformat-number( )function.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:decimal-format>element defines the characters and symbols that are to be used in converting numbers into strings using theformat-number( )function.

XSLT/xsl:element.name: xsl:element
XSLT/xsl:element.description: The<xsl:element>element creates an element in the output document.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:element>element creates an element in the output document.

XSLT/xsl:fallback.name: xsl:fallback
XSLT/xsl:fallback.description: The<xsl:fallback>element specifies what template to use if a given extension (or, eventually, newer version) element is not supported.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:fallback>element specifies what template to use if a given extension (or, eventually, newer version) element is not supported.

XSLT/xsl:for-each.name: xsl:for-each
XSLT/xsl:for-each.description: The<xsl:for-each>element selects a set of nodes and processes each of them in the same way. It is often used to iterate through a set of nodes or to change the current node. If one or more<xsl:sort>elements appear as the children of this element, sorting occurs before processing. Otherwise, nodes ar

XSLT/xsl:if.name: xsl:if
XSLT/xsl:if.description: The<xsl:if>element contains a test attribute and a template. If the test evaluates to true, the template is processed. In this it is similar to an if statement in other languages. To achieve the functionality of an if-then-else statement, however, use the<xsl:choose>element with one<xsl:when>and one

info.excerpt: The<xsl:if>element contains a test attribute and a template. If the test evaluates to true, the template is processed. In this it is similar to an if statement in other languages. To achieve the funct...

XSLT/xsl:import.name: xsl:import
XSLT/xsl:import.description: The<xsl:import>element is a top-level element that serves to import the contents of one stylesheet into another stylesheet. Generally speaking, the contents of the imported stylesheet have a lower import precedence than that of the importing stylesheet. This is in contrast to<xsl:include>where the c

info.excerpt: The<xsl:import>element is a top-level element that serves to import the contents of one stylesheet into another stylesheet. Generally speaking, the contents of the imported stylesheet have a lower imp...

XSLT/xsl:include.name: xsl:include
XSLT/xsl:include.description: The<xsl:include>element merges the contents of one stylesheet with another. Unlike the case of<xsl:import>, the contents of an included stylesheet have exactly the same precedence as the contents of the including stylesheet.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:include>element merges the contents of one stylesheet with another. Unlike the case of<xsl:import>, the contents of an included stylesheet have exactly the same precedence as the contents of t...

XSLT/xsl:key.name: xsl:key
XSLT/xsl:key.description: The<xsl:key>element declares a named key which can be used elsewhere in the stylesheet with thekey( )function.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:key>element declares a named key which can be used elsewhere in the stylesheet with thekey( )function.

XSLT/xsl:message.name: xsl:message
XSLT/xsl:message.description: The<xsl:message>element outputs a message (to the JavaScript Console in NS) and optionally terminates execution of the stylesheet. It can be useful for debugging.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:message>element outputs a message (to the JavaScript Console in NS) and optionally terminates execution of the stylesheet. It can be useful for debugging.

XSLT/xsl:namespace-alias.name: xsl:namespace-alias
XSLT/xsl:namespace-alias.description: The<xsl:namespace-alias>element is a rarely used device that maps a namespace in the stylesheet to a different namespace in the output tree. The most common use for this element is in generating a stylesheet from another stylesheet. To prevent a normallyxsl:-prefixed literal result element (which sh

info.excerpt: The<xsl:namespace-alias>element is a rarely used device that maps a namespace in the stylesheet to a different namespace in the output tree. The most common use for this element is in generating a sty...

XSLT/xsl:number.name: xsl:number
XSLT/xsl:number.description: The<xsl:number>element counts things sequentially. It can also be used to quickly format a number.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:number>element counts things sequentially. It can also be used to quickly format a number.

XSLT/xsl:otherwise.name: xsl:otherwise
XSLT/xsl:otherwise.description: The<xsl:otherwise>element is used to define the action that should be taken when none of the<xsl:when>conditions apply. It is similar to theelseordefaultcase in other programming languages.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:otherwise>element is used to define the action that should be taken when none of the<xsl:when>conditions apply. It is similar to theelseordefaultcase in other programming languages.

XSLT/xsl:output.name: xsl:output
XSLT/xsl:output.description: The<xsl:output>element controls the characteristics of the output document. To function correctly in Netscape, this element, with the method attribute, must be used. As of 7.0,method="text"works as expected.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:output>element controls the characteristics of the output document. To function correctly in Netscape, this element, with the method attribute, must be used. As of 7.0,method="text"works as ex...

XSLT/xsl:param.name: xsl:param
XSLT/xsl:param.description: The<xsl:param>element establishes a parameter by name and, optionally, a default value for that parameter. When used as a top-level element, the parameter is global. When used inside an<xsl:template>element, the parameter is local to that template. In this case it must be the first child element of

info.excerpt: The<xsl:param>element establishes a parameter by name and, optionally, a default value for that parameter. When used as a top-level element, the parameter is global. When used inside an<xsl:template>e...

XSLT/xsl:preserve-space.name: xsl:preserve-space
XSLT/xsl:preserve-space.description: The<xsl:preserve-space>element defines the elements in the source document for which whitespace should be preserved. If there is more than one element, separate the names with a whitespace character. Preserving whitespace is the default setting, so this element only needs to be used to counteract an

info.excerpt: The<xsl:preserve-space>element defines the elements in the source document for which whitespace should be preserved. If there is more than one element, separate the names with a whitespace character. ...

XSLT/xsl:processing-instruction.name: xsl:processing-instruction
XSLT/xsl:processing-instruction.description: The<xsl:processing-instruction>element writes a processing instruction to the output document.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:processing-instruction>element writes a processing instruction to the output document.

XSLT/xsl:sort.name: xsl:sort
XSLT/xsl:sort.description: The<xsl:sort>element defines a sort key for nodes selected by<xsl:apply-templates>or<xsl:for-each>and determines the order in which they are processed.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:sort>element defines a sort key for nodes selected by<xsl:apply-templates>or<xsl:for-each>and determines the order in which they are processed.

XSLT/xsl:strip-space.name: xsl:strip-space
XSLT/xsl:strip-space.description: The<xsl:strip-space>element defines the elements in the source document for which whitespace should be removed.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:strip-space>element defines the elements in the source document for which whitespace should be removed.

XSLT/xsl:stylesheet.name: xsl:stylesheet
XSLT/xsl:stylesheet.description: The<xsl:stylesheet>element (or the equivalent<xsl:transform>element) is the outermost element of a stylesheet.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:stylesheet>element (or the equivalent<xsl:transform>element) is the outermost element of a stylesheet.

XSLT/xsl:template.name: xsl:template
XSLT/xsl:template.description: The<xsl:template>element defines an output producing template. This element must have either the match attribute or the name attribute set.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:template>element defines an output producing template. This element must have either the match attribute or the name attribute set.

XSLT/xsl:text.name: xsl:text
XSLT/xsl:text.description: The<xsl:text>element writes literal text to the output tree. It may contain#PCDATA, literal text, and entity references.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:text>element writes literal text to the output tree. It may contain#PCDATA, literal text, and entity references.

XSLT/xsl:transform.name: xsl:transform
XSLT/xsl:transform.description: The<xsl:transform>element is exactly equivalent to the<xsl:stylesheet>element.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:transform>element is exactly equivalent to the<xsl:stylesheet>element.

XSLT/xsl:value-of.name: xsl:value-of
XSLT/xsl:value-of.description: The<xsl:value-of>element evaluates an XPath expression, converts it to a string, and writes that string to the result tree.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:value-of>element evaluates an XPath expression, converts it to a string, and writes that string to the result tree.

XSLT/xsl:variable.name: xsl:variable
XSLT/xsl:variable.description: The<xsl:variable>element declares a global or local variable in a stylesheet and gives it a value. Because XSLT permits no side-effects, once the value of the variable has been established, it remains the same until the variable goes out of scope

info.excerpt: The<xsl:variable>element declares a global or local variable in a stylesheet and gives it a value. Because XSLT permits no side-effects, once the value of the variable has been established, it remains...

XSLT/xsl:when.name: xsl:when
XSLT/xsl:when.description: The<xsl:when>element always appears within an<xsl:choose>element, acting like a case statement.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:when>element always appears within an<xsl:choose>element, acting like a case statement.

XSLT/xsl:with-param.name: xsl:with-param
XSLT/xsl:with-param.description: The<xsl:with-param>element sets the value of a parameter to be passed into a template.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:with-param>element sets the value of a parameter to be passed into a template.
